rationale: A0 shows root executing 'rm [x].sh' from an interactive terminal (pts1), which is a routine administrative shell-script cleanup action. EV-001 confirms the removal attempt failed with ENOENT (exit code -2, success=no), meaning the target file did not exist at execution time and no file was actually deleted. The combination of a failed no-op deletion with no prior evidence of the file being created, executed, or used for malicious purposes means there is no confirmed harmful outcome. The activity is consistent with a sysadmin cleaning up a script that had already been removed or never existed.
